Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses it 366/2 = 183 times in a leap year. Multiplying the amount used per wash by the number of washes gives 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces of shampoo needed for the entire year. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, she would need to divide the total amount by the bottle size: 45.75 / 14 = 3.267 bottles. Therefore, Brittany needs [number of bottles] to get her through a leap year.

[address] is where she can purchase the shampoo. It's important to note that she should round up to the nearest whole number since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle. This means she will need 4 bottles in total to ensure she has enough shampoo for the entire leap year. Having an extra bottle will also provide a buffer in case she uses more than expected or if there are any unexpected expenses.
